rationale: In the citing paper, Kirmani and Zhu (2007) appears only in a grouped background citation used to support a broad claim that advertising contexts can yield different outcomes than other communication contexts. The paper does not separately discuss Kirmani and Zhu's regulatory-focus or persuasion-knowledge findings, and it does not build hypotheses, measures, or analyses directly from that study. Because the citation serves as general literature support without a distinct functional role in this article's core model, it is best classified as a passing mention.
